_derivation.description: Real, server-computed HMAC-SHA256 fixture vectors proving byte-for-byte cross-language compatibility between axiam_sdk.amqp._hmac.verify_hmac (Python) and crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs::sign_payload (Rust). Each vector's hmac_signature was NOT hand-written — it was emitted by the canonical Rust signer via a throwaway #[test] harness, then independently cross-checked with Python's hmac.new(...).hexdigest() before being recorded here. Updated for NEW-4 (AMQP replay protection, v2): every message now carries key_version=2, nonce, and issued_at, positioned per crates/axiam-amqp/tests/fixtures/v2_reference_vectors.json's field_order (after the schema-specific fields, before hmac_signature); signatures were recomputed with the exact same signing key over the v2-shaped canonical bytes (Jackson ObjectMapper.writeValueAsBytes, verified byte-identical to the Rust/Python canonicalization for this ASCII-only payload).
_derivation.reproduction_command: cd /home/user/axiam && (temporarily add the `emit_python_sdk_hmac_fixture` test to crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs's `tests` module, mirroring the AuthzRequest/AuditEventMessage construction below) && cargo test -p axiam-amqp emit_python_sdk_hmac_fixture -- --nocapture
_derivation.note: The field order in every 'message' object below is the Rust struct DECLARATION order (see AuthzRequest / AuditEventMessage in crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs), NOT alphabetical order — e.g. AuthzRequest emits tenant_id before subject_id, and action before resource_id, with key_version/nonce/issued_at appended last (before hmac_signature). This confirms 19-RESEARCH.md's Pitfall 2 finding: canonicalization MUST preserve wire/insertion order (json.dumps WITHOUT sort_keys), not sort keys alphabetically. This fixture exercises Hmac.verify's raw-key structural behavior (ordering/tamper/edge cases); crates/axiam-amqp/tests/fixtures/v2_reference_vectors.json is the authoritative HKDF-subkey cross-language vector for NEW-4.

vectors.name: authz_request_tampered_action
vectors.note: Same signature as authz_request_valid but with action mutated from 'read' to 'write' after signing — proves verification is non-vacuous.

vectors.name: audit_event_wrong_key
vectors.note: Same body/signature as audit_event_valid but verified against a different signing key (hex-decodes to 'wrong-key-for-test-only') — proves the wrong key is rejected.

vectors.name: missing_hmac_signature
vectors.note: hmac_signature field entirely absent — strict mode default (CONTRACT.md §8.3) rejects, never silently accepts.

vectors.name: non_hex_signature
vectors.note: Non-hex signature value must be rejected without raising.

vectors.name: wrong_length_signature
vectors.note: Truncated (wrong-length) hex signature must be rejected without raising.
